name: Formaldehyde sensor (HxlR + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects toxic formaldehyde in water, air, and materials. The native B. subtilis HxlR formaldehyde regulator gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in spore-forming Bacillus subtilis 168.

input.detects: Formaldehyde in water, off-gassing materials, and indoor air

sensing.sensorMechanism: HxlR activates the hxlAB formaldehyde-detoxification operon in response to formaldehyde.
sensing.signalFlow: HCHO -> HxlR activates PhxlA -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with formaldehyde.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

performance.notes: HxlR formaldehyde sensing and CRISPRa are validated separately; integrated device is a design template.

safety.notes: Spores allow ambient storage and field reconstitution.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a weak reporter promoter; check B. subtilis off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: PhxlA -> sgRNA (native HxlR). TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> sfGFP. Integrate at amyE.

buildSteps.title: Transform B. subtilis 168
buildSteps.description: Natural competence; select; confirm formaldehyde-dependent activation.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Reconstitute spores; add sample + formaldehyde standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native B. subtilis HxlR formaldehyde sensor with CRISPRa
partsSources: Yurimoto et al. / B. subtilis HxlR formaldehyde regulation
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
